title.en: CodeChat-Eval: Evaluating Large Language Models in Multi-Turn Code Refinement Dialogues
title.zh: CodeChat-Eval：评估大语言模型在多轮代码优化对话中的表现

abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly used in software engineering to generate and refine code. In practice, developers often continue from an initial code generation request with follow-up refinement instructions, such as requests to improve style, restructure implementation, or change the execution strategy while preserving the intended behaviour. However, existing benchmarks generally omit this multi-turn code refinement dialogue setting and therefore cannot evaluate whether LLMs maintain functional correctness, i.e., whether the refined code still passes the test suite for the original task. To address this limitation, we introduce CodeChat-Eval, an evaluation framework that constructs evaluation sessions from multi-turn code refinement dialogues using a dynamic instruction selection algorithm. Our empirical study on open-weight and proprietary LLMs observes a statistically significant decrease ranging from 19.2% (GPT-5 Nano) to 69.2% (Llama 3.1 8B) in functional correctness over multi-turn refinement. The largest correctness drops are associated with logic-level refinements and additive change requests. These findings indicate that LLMs struggle to maintain functional correctness during multi-turn code refinement dialogues, and highlight the need for benchmarks that evaluate functionality-preserving refinement beyond single-turn generation.

relevance.en: This paper extends your evaluation focus beyond single-turn pass@k by introducing a multi-turn refinement benchmark that measures functional correctness across iterative dialogues, directly addressing your interest in realistic, integration-aware benchmarks for code LLMs.
relevance.zh: 本文通过引入一个多轮优化基准测试，将您的评估焦点从单轮pass@k扩展到迭代对话中的功能正确性测量，直接回应了您对代码大语言模型进行真实、集成感知基准测试的兴趣。

figures.label: Figure 1

caption.en:
A motivating example of multi-turn code refinement that demonstrates
the scenarios when LLMs fail to adhere to a developer’s instruction or fail to
maintain the functional correctness (i.e., do not pass the test suite).
caption.zh: 一个多轮代码优化的激励示例，展示了当大语言模型未能遵循开发者指令或未能保持功能正确性（即未通过测试套件）时的场景。

figures.reference_text: I. INTRODUCTION Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into real-world software development workflows, supporting both code generation and refinement [1]. These interactions often unfold over multiple turns, as developers iteratively refine generated code through follow-up instructions [2]. A typical dialogue begins with an initial code-generation request (e.g., “write a Python function to remove odd numbers from a given list”), followed by refinement instructions that adapt the code to individual preferences or team coding standards (e.g., “remove all comments”). Ideally, such LLMs must revise the code as per the developer-preferred instructions (defined as instruction adher- ence), while maintaining the functional correctness of the code (i.e., the generated code fully passes the given test suite) [2]– [5]. However, in follow-up turns, there are many cases that LLMs may fail to adhere to the developer’s instruction (i.e., a given instruction is not applied in the follow-up turn) or fail to maintain the functional correctness. As illustrated in our motivating example (see Fig. 1), the LLM already fails to adhere to the developer’s instruction in the second turn

figures.label: Figure 2
figures.reference_text:
CodeChat-Eval is an evaluation framework designed to assess LLMs in multi-turn code refinement dialogues. As shown in Fig. 2, first, the raw instructions are cleaned and categorised into different types to form an instruction pool. The evaluation agenda is introduced to guide the generation of each evaluation session and achieves a balanced distribution of different types of instructions. Using dynamic instruction selection algorithm, CodeChat-Eval then adaptively selects the applicable refinement instruction as the input of the follow- up turn. Finally, the verification step evaluates functional correctness and records instruction adherence as a supporting measure, forming the whole evaluation session.

The scope is defined as the depth of intervention required to fulfil the code refinement described in the instruc- tion. brief_summary.en:
Current code generation benchmarks evaluate LLMs on single-turn tasks, where a model produces code from a description and is tested once. In practice, developers refine code through multi-turn dialogues—asking for style improvements, logic changes, or feature additions—while expecting the original functionality to remain intact. This critical gap means we don't know whether LLMs can maintain functional correctness across iterative refinements, limiting their reliability in real-world software engineering workflows.

To address this, we introduce CodeChat-Eval, an evaluation framework that simulates multi-turn code refinement using a dynamic instruction selection algorithm. It constructs 10-turn sessions from categorized instructions (by scope and change type) and tests whether refined code still passes the original test suite. Across six LLMs, functional correctness dropped significantly—from 19.2% (GPT-5 Nano) to 69.2% (Llama 3.1 8B)—with the largest declines caused by logic-level refinements and additive changes. These results reveal that even state-of-the-art models struggle to preserve functionality during iterative refinement, highlighting the urgent need for benchmarks and training strategies that address this real-world challenge.
brief_summary.zh:
当前的代码生成基准测试仅评估大语言模型在单轮任务中的表现，即模型根据描述生成代码并一次性测试。然而在实际开发中，开发者通过多轮对话来优化代码——要求改进风格、修改逻辑或添加功能——同时期望原有功能保持不变。这一关键差距意味着我们无法得知大语言模型能否在迭代优化过程中保持功能正确性，从而限制了它们在真实软件工程工作流中的可靠性。

为解决这一问题，我们提出了CodeChat-Eval，一个通过动态指令选择算法模拟多轮代码优化的评估框架。该框架从分类指令（按作用域和变更类型）构建10轮会话，并测试优化后的代码是否仍能通过原始测试套件。在六个大语言模型上，功能正确性显著下降——从19.2%（GPT-5 Nano）到69.2%（Llama 3.1 8B）——其中最大的下降由逻辑级优化和新增变更引起。这些结果表明，即使是最先进的模型在迭代优化中也难以保持功能正确性，凸显了针对这一现实挑战的基准测试和训练策略的迫切需求。

problem.en: Existing benchmarks for evaluating LLMs in code generation focus exclusively on single-turn interactions, where the model produces code from a natural language description and is tested against a suite of unit tests. In real-world software development, however, developers engage in multi-turn dialogues, starting with an initial code request and following up with refinement instructions—such as improving style, restructuring, or changing execution strategy—while expecting the code to remain functionally correct. This practical setting is entirely overlooked by current evaluation frameworks, creating a critical gap: it is unknown whether LLMs can maintain functional correctness across multiple refinement turns. Without such evaluation, deployers of LLM-based coding assistants cannot assess their reliability in iterative development workflows, and researchers lack a benchmark to drive progress in functionality-preserving code refinement.
problem.zh: 现有的评估大语言模型代码生成的基准测试仅关注单轮交互，即模型根据自然语言描述生成代码，并通过一组单元测试进行测试。然而，在实际软件开发中，开发者进行多轮对话，从初始代码请求开始，随后提出优化指令——例如改进风格、重构或改变执行策略——同时期望代码保持功能正确。这种实际设置完全被当前评估框架忽视，造成了一个关键空白：我们不知道大语言模型能否在多个优化轮次中保持功能正确性。缺乏此类评估，基于大语言模型的编码助手的部署者无法评估其在迭代开发工作流中的可靠性，而研究人员也缺乏推动功能保持优化进步的基准测试。

solution.en: CodeChat-Eval is a novel evaluation framework specifically designed for multi-turn code refinement dialogues. Its core contribution is a dynamic instruction selection algorithm that constructs evaluation sessions from a pool of categorized instructions, ensuring a balanced and realistic distribution of refinement types (scope and change). Unlike prior work that tests only initial code generation, CodeChat-Eval systematically measures functional correctness over multiple turns by checking whether refined code still passes the original test suite. The key insight is that evaluation must simulate the interactive nature of real refinement—where instructions vary in scope (line-level, logic-level, design-level) and change operation (add, remove, modify)—and that instruction selection should be automated and balanced to avoid bias toward any single category.
solution.zh: CodeChat-Eval是一个专门为多轮代码优化对话设计的新型评估框架。其核心贡献是一个动态指令选择算法，该算法从分类指令池中构建评估会话，确保优化类型（作用域和变更）的平衡和真实分布。与仅测试初始代码生成的先前工作不同，CodeChat-Eval通过检查优化后的代码是否仍能通过原始测试套件，系统地测量多轮中的功能正确性。关键见解是评估必须模拟真实优化的交互性质——其中指令在作用域（行级、逻辑级、设计级）和变更操作（添加、删除、修改）上各不相同——并且指令选择应自动化和平衡，以避免偏向任何单一类别。

methodology.en: The CodeChat-Eval pipeline begins by collecting raw refinement instructions from developer-LLM interaction logs and manually cleaning and categorizing them into a structured instruction pool. Each instruction is labeled by scope (line, logic, design) and change (add, remove, modify). An 'evaluation agenda' is then defined, specifying the desired number of turns (e.g., 10) and a target distribution across instruction types. The dynamic instruction selection algorithm, at each turn, chooses an instruction from the pool that best satisfies the remaining distribution targets, while also ensuring diversity and avoiding repetition. The initial turn (Turn 0) is a standard code generation request from the HumanEval or MBPP dataset; subsequent turns apply selected refinement instructions to the previously generated code. For each turn, the LLM's output is executed against the original task's test suite to determine functional correctness (pass/fail). The framework supports both open-weight and proprietary LLMs. Key hyperparameters include the number of turns (9 refinement turns after initial generation) and the target balance (e.g., 30% line, 40% logic, 30% design; equal thirds for add/remove/modify). The output is a per-turn correctness trace for each evaluation session.
methodology.zh: CodeChat-Eval流程首先从开发者-大语言模型交互日志中收集原始优化指令，并手动清理和分类到结构化指令池中。每条指令按作用域（行、逻辑、设计）和变更（添加、删除、修改）进行标记。然后定义“评估议程”，指定期望的轮次数（例如10轮）和指令类型的目标分布。动态指令选择算法在每一轮从池中选择一条最能满足剩余分布目标的指令，同时确保多样性和避免重复。初始轮（第0轮）是来自HumanEval或MBPP数据集的标准代码生成请求；后续轮次将选定的优化指令应用于先前生成的代码。对于每一轮，大语言模型的输出针对原始任务的测试套件执行，以确定功能正确性（通过/失败）。该框架支持开放权重和专有大语言模型。关键超参数包括轮次数（初始生成后的9个优化轮次）和目标平衡（例如30%行级、40%逻辑级、30%设计级；添加/删除/修改各占三分之一）。输出是每个评估会话的逐轮正确性轨迹。

experiments.en: Experiments were conducted on two standard code generation benchmarks: HumanEval (164 tasks) and MBPP (500 tasks). Six LLMs were evaluated: GPT-4, GPT-5 Nano, GPT-5 Pro, Llama 3.1 8B, Llama 3.1 70B, and Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B. For each task, an evaluation session of 10 turns was run (1 initial generation + 9 refinements). The primary metric was functional correctness (percentage of tasks passing all tests). Results showed a statistically significant decrease in correctness over turns across all models, ranging from 19.2% drop (GPT-5 Nano: from 94.5% to 75.3%) to 69.2% drop (Llama 3.1 8B: from 52.4% to 16.2%). Ablation analyses examined regression rates (Pass-to-Fail) by instruction scope and change. Logic-level refinements caused the highest regression (average 0.25 on HumanEval vs. 0.12 for line-level). Additive changes (adding features/code) also caused high regression (average 0.163 on HumanEval, 0.183 on MBPP). Instruction adherence (whether the model followed the instruction) was high (>85% for most models) but did not correlate strongly with correctness, indicating that correctness degradation is not simply due to ignoring instructions. Self-correction (Fail-to-Pass) rates were low, with Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B reaching the highest at 12.6%.
experiments.zh: 实验在两个标准代码生成基准测试上进行：HumanEval（164个任务）和MBPP（500个任务）。评估了六个大语言模型：GPT-4、GPT-5 Nano、GPT-5 Pro、Llama 3.1 8B、Llama 3.1 70B和Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B。对于每个任务，运行一个10轮的评估会话（1次初始生成+9次优化）。主要指标是功能正确性（通过所有测试的任务百分比）。结果显示所有模型在轮次中正确性均出现统计显著下降，下降幅度从19.2%（GPT-5 Nano：从94.5%降至75.3%）到69.2%（Llama 3.1 8B：从52.4%降至16.2%）。消融分析按指令作用域和变更检查了回归率（通过→失败）。逻辑级优化导致最高的回归（HumanEval上平均0.25，而行级为0.12）。新增变更（添加功能/代码）也导致高回归（HumanEval上平均0.163，MBPP上0.183）。指令遵循度（模型是否遵循指令）很高（大多数模型>85%），但与正确性相关性不强，表明正确性退化并非仅仅由于忽略指令。自我修正（失败→通过）率较低，Qwen 2.5 Coder 14B最高达到12.6%。

conclusion.en: The study finds that all evaluated LLMs suffer a statistically significant decline in functional correctness over multi-turn code refinement, with the largest drops caused by logic-level and additive change requests. While models maintain high instruction adherence, they fail to integrate changes without breaking existing tests, revealing a critical gap in current models' robustness for iterative software engineering tasks. The findings underscore the need for benchmarks like CodeChat-Eval to drive improvements, but the paper does not explore root causes or propose mitigation strategies, nor does it address repository-level or cross-file scenarios. For researchers, this means that even state-of-the-art LLMs are unreliable for multi-turn code refinement, and future work must focus on training or prompting techniques that preserve functionality across dialogue turns.
conclusion.zh: 研究发现所有评估的大语言模型在多轮代码优化中功能正确性均出现统计显著下降，最大的下降由逻辑级和新增变更请求引起。虽然模型保持高指令遵循度，但它们无法在不破坏现有测试的情况下整合变更，揭示了当前模型在迭代软件工程任务中鲁棒性的关键差距。研究结果强调了像CodeChat-Eval这样的基准测试推动改进的必要性，但论文未探索根本原因或提出缓解策略，也未涉及仓库级或跨文件场景。对于研究人员而言，这意味着即使是最先进的大语言模型在多轮代码优化中也不可靠，未来工作必须专注于在对话轮次中保持功能正确性的训练或提示技术。

question.en: How does the decline in functional correctness over multi-turn refinement compare across models that underwent different post-training techniques, and does the paper provide any insight into which post-training strategies (e.g., RL with execution feedback) might mitigate this degradation?
question.zh: 在多轮优化过程中，功能正确性的下降幅度在不同后训练技术的模型之间如何比较？论文是否提供了关于哪些后训练策略（例如带执行反馈的强化学习）可能缓解这种退化的见解？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user’s interest in post-training techniques and their impact on correctness. The paper compares multiple LLMs, including proprietary ones likely fine-tuned with RLHF/execution feedback, but does not analyze post-training strategies explicitly. Asking this probes whether the benchmark could reveal differences attributable to training methods, which is a gap the user would want to understand.
reason.zh: 该问题直接针对用户对后训练技术及其对正确性影响的兴趣。论文比较了多个大语言模型，包括可能经过RLHF/执行反馈微调的专有模型，但并未明确分析后训练策略。提出此问题旨在探究该基准测试是否能揭示因训练方法不同而产生的差异，这是用户希望理解的空白。

question.en: Given that the benchmark uses isolated code tasks (HumanEval, MBPP), how could the CodeChat-Eval framework be extended to repository-level scenarios where multi-turn refinement must maintain correctness across interdependent files and longer contexts?
question.zh: 鉴于该基准测试使用孤立的代码任务（HumanEval、MBPP），CodeChat-Eval框架如何扩展到仓库级场景，其中多轮优化必须保持跨相互依赖文件和更长上下文的正确性？

reason.en: This question bridges the paper’s multi-turn evaluation focus with the user’s core interest in repository-level code generation and long-context handling. It asks the user to consider how the dynamic instruction selection algorithm could scale to real-world codebases, a step toward more comprehensive benchmarks that measure integration with existing code.
reason.zh: 该问题将论文的多轮评估焦点与用户对仓库级代码生成和长上下文处理的核心兴趣联系起来。它要求用户考虑动态指令选择算法如何扩展到真实代码库，这是迈向更全面基准测试（衡量与现有代码的集成）的一步。

question.en: What specific types of refinement instructions cause the largest functional correctness drops (logic-level, additive changes), and does the paper suggest how evaluation methodologies like CodeChat-Eval could be used to guide post-training data selection to improve robustness?
question.zh: 哪些特定类型的优化指令会导致最大的功能正确性下降（逻辑级、新增变更）？论文是否建议像CodeChat-Eval这样的评估方法可用于指导后训练数据选择以提高鲁棒性？

reason.en: This question connects the paper’s findings on instruction categories to the user’s interest in post-training improvement (e.g., SFT or RL with execution feedback). It asks whether the benchmark can inform training data curation, a practical implication for those developing models for real-world SE tasks.
reason.zh: 该问题将论文关于指令类别的发现与用户对后训练改进（例如SFT或带执行反馈的强化学习）的兴趣联系起来。它询问该基准测试是否能为训练数据筛选提供信息，这对开发面向真实软件工程任务的模型具有实际意义。

question.en: How does the decline in functional correctness over multi-turn refinement compare across models that underwent different post-training techniques, and does the paper provide any insight into which post-training strategies (e.g., RL with execution feedback) might mitigate this degradation?
question.zh: 在多轮优化过程中，功能正确性的下降幅度在不同后训练技术的模型之间如何比较？论文是否提供了关于哪些后训练策略（例如带执行反馈的强化学习）可能缓解这种退化的见解？

reason.en: This question directly targets the user’s interest in post-training techniques and their impact on correctness. The paper compares multiple LLMs, including proprietary ones likely fine-tuned with RLHF/execution feedback, but does not analyze post-training strategies explicitly. Asking this probes whether the benchmark could reveal differences attributable to training methods, which is a gap the user would want to understand.
reason.zh: 该问题直接针对用户对后训练技术及其对正确性影响的兴趣。论文比较了多个大语言模型，包括可能经过RLHF/执行反馈微调的专有模型，但并未明确分析后训练策略。提出此问题旨在探究该基准测试是否能揭示因训练方法不同而产生的差异，这是用户希望理解的空白。

answer.en: The paper reports a statistically significant decline in functional correctness over multi-turn refinement for all models tested, but it does not systematically compare models based on their post-training techniques. The abstract notes that the drop ranges from “19.2% (GPT-5 Nano) to 69.2% (Llama 3.1 8B),” but no analysis links these differences to training methods such as RLHF or execution feedback. The paper focuses on the multi-turn setting and states that “existing benchmarks generally omit this multi-turn code refinement dialogue setting” and calls for “benchmarks that evaluate functionality-preserving refinement beyond single-turn generation.” It does not provide insight into which post-training strategies might mitigate degradation; the question is not addressed in the paper.
answer.zh: 论文报告了所有测试模型在多轮优化中功能正确性均出现统计显著下降，但并未根据后训练技术对模型进行系统比较。摘要指出下降幅度从“19.2%（GPT-5 Nano）到69.2%（Llama 3.1 8B）”，但未将这些差异与RLHF或执行反馈等训练方法联系起来。论文聚焦于多轮设置，指出“现有基准测试通常忽略这种多轮代码优化对话设置”，并呼吁“评估超越单轮生成的功能保持优化的基准测试”。论文未提供关于哪些后训练策略可能缓解退化的见解；该问题在论文中未得到解答。

question.en: Given that the benchmark uses isolated code tasks (HumanEval, MBPP), how could the CodeChat-Eval framework be extended to repository-level scenarios where multi-turn refinement must maintain correctness across interdependent files and longer contexts?
question.zh: 鉴于该基准测试使用孤立的代码任务（HumanEval、MBPP），CodeChat-Eval框架如何扩展到仓库级场景，其中多轮优化必须保持跨相互依赖文件和更长上下文的正确性？

reason.en: This question bridges the paper’s multi-turn evaluation focus with the user’s core interest in repository-level code generation and long-context handling. It asks the user to consider how the dynamic instruction selection algorithm could scale to real-world codebases, a step toward more comprehensive benchmarks that measure integration with existing code.
reason.zh: 该问题将论文的多轮评估焦点与用户对仓库级代码生成和长上下文处理的核心兴趣联系起来。它要求用户考虑动态指令选择算法如何扩展到真实代码库，这是迈向更全面基准测试（衡量与现有代码的集成）的一步。

answer.en: The CodeChat-Eval paper does not directly address extending its framework to repository-level scenarios, as its benchmark is explicitly based on isolated code tasks: "existing benchmarks generally omit this multi-turn code refinement dialogue setting" and it uses HumanEval and MBPP, which are single-file, isolated tasks. However, the paper's core mechanism—the "dynamic instruction selection algorithm"—could be adapted by treating each file in a repository as an independent evaluation session while adding cross-file correctness checks. The authors note that "the largest correctness drops are associated with logic-level refinements and additive change requests," implying that in a repository setting, similar refinement types would need to track dependencies across files (e.g., function signatures, shared data structures) to ensure multi-turn changes don't break interdependent modules. The abstract states LLMs "struggle to maintain functional correctness during multi-turn code refinement dialogues," a problem that would amplify in repository contexts due to longer contexts and inter-file dependencies. Thus, extending the framework would require augmenting the dynamic instruction selection to consider file adjacency and static analysis of cross-file impacts, a step the paper does not explore.
answer.zh: CodeChat-Eval论文未直接解决将其框架扩展到仓库级场景的问题，因为其基准测试明确基于孤立代码任务：“现有基准测试通常忽略这种多轮代码优化对话设置”，并且它使用HumanEval和MBPP，这些是单文件、孤立的任务。然而，论文的核心机制——“动态指令选择算法”——可以通过将仓库中的每个文件视为独立评估会话并添加跨文件正确性检查来适应。作者指出“最大的正确性下降与逻辑级优化和新增变更请求相关”，这意味着在仓库设置中，类似的优化类型需要跟踪跨文件的依赖关系（例如函数签名、共享数据结构），以确保多轮变更不会破坏相互依赖的模块。摘要指出大语言模型“在多轮代码优化对话中难以保持功能正确性”，这一问题在仓库上下文中会因更长的上下文和跨文件依赖而加剧。因此，扩展该框架需要增强动态指令选择以考虑文件邻接和跨文件影响的静态分析，这是论文未探索的步骤。

question.en: What specific types of refinement instructions cause the largest functional correctness drops (logic-level, additive changes), and does the paper suggest how evaluation methodologies like CodeChat-Eval could be used to guide post-training data selection to improve robustness?
question.zh: 哪些特定类型的优化指令会导致最大的功能正确性下降（逻辑级、新增变更）？论文是否建议像CodeChat-Eval这样的评估方法可用于指导后训练数据选择以提高鲁棒性？

reason.en: This question connects the paper’s findings on instruction categories to the user’s interest in post-training improvement (e.g., SFT or RL with execution feedback). It asks whether the benchmark can inform training data curation, a practical implication for those developing models for real-world SE tasks.
reason.zh: 该问题将论文关于指令类别的发现与用户对后训练改进（例如SFT或带执行反馈的强化学习）的兴趣联系起来。它询问该基准测试是否能为训练数据筛选提供信息，这对开发面向真实软件工程任务的模型具有实际意义。

answer.en: The paper specifically identifies that the largest functional correctness drops are caused by "logic-level refinements and additive change requests." This is directly stated in the abstract: "The largest correctness drops are associated with logic-level refinements and additive change requests." Regarding whether CodeChat-Eval could guide post-training data selection to improve robustness, the paper does not explicitly discuss such use. It primarily focuses on evaluating functionality and highlighting the challenge, stating the findings "highlight the need for benchmarks that evaluate functionality-preserving refinement beyond single-turn generation." No concrete methodology for using the benchmark to curate training data is proposed or implied.
answer.zh: 论文明确指出最大的功能正确性下降是由“逻辑级优化和新增变更请求”引起的。这在摘要中直接陈述：“最大的正确性下降与逻辑级优化和新增变更请求相关”。关于CodeChat-Eval是否可用于指导后训练数据选择以提高鲁棒性，论文并未明确讨论此类用途。它主要侧重于评估功能并强调挑战，指出研究结果“凸显了对评估超越单轮生成的功能保持优化的基准测试的需求”。论文未提出或暗示使用该基准测试来筛选训练数据的具体方法。
